configurations.name: 1. CCM Databases Only (Docker)
configurations.description: Start only PostgreSQL + MongoDB in Docker. Use this as the first step of the hybrid local dev workflow. Databases are then accessible at localhost:5432 and localhost:27017.

configurations.name: 2. CCM API (local dev — nodemon + tsx)
configurations.description: Run the API directly on the host with hot-reload via nodemon. Loads .env + .env.local so POSTGRES_HOST and MONGO_HOST resolve to localhost. Requires databases to be running (config 1 or full Docker stack).

configurations.name: 3. CCM Web (local dev — Vite HMR)
configurations.description: Run the Vite dev server directly on the host. Changes to src/ reflect in the browser in under 1 second via HMR. /api/* requests are proxied to localhost:3000. Requires the API to be running (config 2 or full Docker stack).

configurations.name: 4. CCM Full Stack — fast start (Docker, no rebuild)
configurations.description: Start all 4 containers using already-built images. Use when you have confirmed changes locally and want to run the full stack without rebuilding. App is served at http://localhost:8080.

configurations.name: 5. CCM Full Stack — with build (Docker)
configurations.description: Build all images and start all 4 containers. Use after Dockerfile changes, new npm dependencies, or for a clean first-run. App is served at http://localhost:8080. This is the slowest option — only use when a rebuild is actually needed.

configurations.name: 6. CCM Web Preview (Vite production build)
configurations.description: Build the Vite app in production mode and serve it locally on port 4173. Use to verify the production bundle before Docker build. Requires a prior 'npm run build' in apps/web.
